title: analytics — trait composition
description: One owned trait — `EmitsAnalyticsEvent` — mixed into any domain aggregate that wants to emit analytics events from its own methods (rare; usually done via listeners). Complements the primary capture path (domain event → attribute-registered capturer). Not a wrapper for a vendor package.

owned.description: Mixed into a domain aggregate that wants to fire analytics events INLINE from its methods. Adds `->emitAnalytics(string $eventName, string $category, array $properties = [], ?string $kind = 'track'): void`. Under the hood this dispatches an `AnalyticsEventCaptured` event with the aggregate as the subject. Rare — the standard path is a Domain Event + a listener registered via #[AsAnalyticsEventCapturer]. Inline emission is reserved for cases where the domain event doesn't carry enough context for the analytics event (e.g. multi-step forms where each step is its own analytics event but the aggregate only fires one 'FormSubmitted' domain event at the end).
usedBy: future consumers (rarely; prefer the listener path)
owned.note: Trait adds NO columns. Subject identity is the aggregate's own primary key + class. The analytics event fires ASYNCHRONOUSLY (queued CaptureAnalyticsEventJob) so it never blocks the aggregate's method path.

framework.reason: AnalyticsProviderConfig + AnalyticsIdentity only. Events + deliveries are hard-purged at retention boundary.

foundation.consumed_by: AnalyticsProviderConfig + AnalyticsIdentity + AnalyticsEvent. NOT AnalyticsDelivery (parent event carries the compliance trail; delivery deltas would double-log volume).

tenancy.reason: Every entity carries tenant_id. Applied via global scope + auto-fill on save. Order matters: BelongsToTenant MUST come first among tenancy-related traits.

attribution.consumed_by: AnalyticsEvent — the trait's boot hook fills attribution jsonb at creating from the active AttributionContext.

compliance.note: NOT applied. Analytics events snapshot consent at CREATE time into a jsonb column — not a runtime lookup. The consent check happens in the capture path (AnalyticsEventObserver.creating), not on model-level trait composition.

compliance.consumed_by: All four entities via the retention.json declaration; the trait provides the runtime helper `->retentionEffective()`.

analytics_event.notes: NO SoftDeletes — events are hard-purged at retention boundary (no soft-delete on immutable ledger). HasAuditable writes state transitions to audit but not the full property blob (audit's own retention is longer than analytics's).

analytics_delivery.notes: NO SoftDeletes. NO HasAuditable (parent AnalyticsEvent carries the compliance trail; delivery-level deltas are too high-volume for audit).

analytics_provider_config.notes: SoftDeletes so a deactivated config's historical audit trail remains browsable during litigation retros. HasAuditable writes every config change (credential rotation, sampling change, enabled_event_types change).

analytics_identity.notes: SoftDeletes so a deidentified identity can be inspected during a subject-access request. HasAuditable writes identify() + alias() + deidentify() transitions.
